message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: My task (remove temperature modulation, add deterministic cache salt) only touches grounded_proposer.py. What files does your feature need? If it also touches grounded_proposer.py we need to coordinate closely on non-overlapping regions or sequence our edits.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: My feature (weighted tip selection) only touches grounded_proposer.py. Let's see if yours touches the same file - if so we need to carefully split functions/sections.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
your_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: We both need this one file, so let's split by function/region instead of by filename. I (agent1) will only touch: DescribeProgram, DescribeModule signature classes (lines ~28-72), generate_instruction_class/GenerateSingleModuleInstruction (add cache_salt InputField only, ~75-132), GenerateModuleInstruction.forward (add cache_salt param and pass-through to describe_program/describe_module/generate_module_instruction calls, ~166-267), and propose_instruction_for_predictor (remove temperature modulation, add self._cache_salt() new method + compute/pass cache_salt, ~388-442). I will NOT touch the TIPS dict (lines 17-24) or propose_instructions_for_program's tip-selection logic (lines ~326-386) - that's yours for weighted tip selection. Only shared line is the call site in propose_instructions_for_program where propose_instruction_for_predictor is invoked (~373-384) - I won't add args there since cache_salt is computed inside propose_instruction_for_predictor itself, not passed in. Let me know if weighted tip selection needs anything outside lines 17-24 and 326-386.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: We both touch this file but different methods - should be safe if we stick to disjoint regions. I will ONLY touch: (1) __init__ signature/body to add a new 'tip_weights' param + self.tip_weights assignment, appended as the LAST param before the closing paren and the LAST line in __init__ body right after 'self.rng = rng or random' (before the prompt_model line) (2) the tip-selection block inside propose_instructions_for_program (lines ~360-371, the 'if self.set_tip_randomly' block) (3) a new helper method _select_tip_with_weights that I will append at the very end of the class, after propose_instruction_for_predictor. Please keep propose_instruction_for_predictor's body (temp/cache-salt logic, lines ~388-440) as your exclusive territory. If you also need an __init__ param, please add it after my assignment line so we don't collide on the same lines. Sound OK? If yes, reply ACCEPT and we can both start editing.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: ACCEPT. Confirming split: you own __init__'s new tip_weights param/assignment (after 'self.rng = rng or random'), the tip-selection block in propose_instructions_for_program (~360-371), and your new _select_tip_with_weights method placed at the END of the file/class (after propose_instruction_for_predictor). I own propose_instruction_for_predictor's body (remove temp modulation, add cache-salt logic) plus the three signature classes and GenerateModuleInstruction.forward for the cache_salt field/param. One placement note to avoid collision: I also need to add ONE new method (_cache_salt) to GroundedProposer - I will insert it directly BEFORE propose_instruction_for_predictor (i.e., between propose_instructions_for_program and propose_instruction_for_predictor), NOT at the end of the file, so it won't collide with your _select_tip_with_weights being appended after propose_instruction_for_predictor. I won't touch __init__, TIPS dict, or the tip-selection block. Starting implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Agreed. To be precise about my scope: I will touch (a) __init__ signature/body (lines ~272-324) to add a 'tip_weights' param + self.tip_weights assignment - I won't touch DescribeProgram/DescribeModule/generate_instruction_class/GenerateModuleInstruction.forward, so no conflict there; (b) TIPS dict (17-24) - read-only, not modifying it; (c) propose_instructions_for_program tip-selection block (~326-386); (d) a new helper method _select_tip_with_weights, which I'll insert as pure addition right after the 'return proposed_instructions' line (end of propose_instructions_for_program) and BEFORE the 'def propose_instruction_for_predictor(' line - I will not touch that def line or anything inside propose_instruction_for_predictor's body, that stays 100% yours. Starting edits now.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Wait - collision: you said _select_tip_with_weights goes right after 'return proposed_instructions' and BEFORE 'def propose_instruction_for_predictor(' - that's the exact same insertion point I claimed for my _cache_salt method. Resolving: I'll move mine to the END of the file instead, i.e. AFTER propose_instruction_for_predictor's closing (after the final 'return strip_prefix(proposed_instruction)' line). So final layout top-to-bottom: propose_instructions_for_program -> your _select_tip_with_weights -> propose_instruction_for_predictor (mine, body only) -> my new _cache_salt method at the very end of the class/file. This keeps our insertions non-adjacent. Confirm and I'll start editing.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Finished my part: added tip_weights param to __init__, weighted selection in propose_instructions_for_program's tip-selection block, and _select_tip_with_weights() helper inserted right after 'return proposed_instructions' and before your propose_instruction_for_predictor def. Did not touch __init__ body beyond the new param/assignment, TIPS dict, or propose_instruction_for_predictor. Ready for you to layer your changes in.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Finished my part: removed temperature modulation from propose_instruction_for_predictor (T kept unused for API compat), added _cache_salt() method placed after propose_instruction_for_predictor at end of class, added cache_salt InputField to DescribeProgram/DescribeModule/GenerateSingleModuleInstruction signatures, threaded cache_salt through GenerateModuleInstruction.forward and its three signature calls. Did not touch __init__, TIPS dict, or the tip-selection block in propose_instructions_for_program. Existing tests pass. Writing my patch.txt now.
